column8: The children (to run) at the stadium every day.
column9: They (to sing) merrily at the moment.
column10: We ... just ... (to learn) a new song!
column12: At seven o’clock yesterday I (to go) to the theatre.
column13: What you (to do) at 5 o’clock yesterday? — I (to play) the piano.
column14: We (to play) in the yard the whole evening yesterday.
column15: I (not to wash) the dishes from six till seven yesterday.
column16: She (to cook) the whole day yesterday.
column17: We (to wash) the floor in our flat from three till four yesterday.
column18: He (to read) a book the whole evening yesterday.
column19: They (to sleep) when you came home.
